title: caltha palustris subsp. laeta
intro: Caltha palustris subsp. laeta, also known as Marsh Marigold, is a plant species that belongs to the buttercup family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and inflammation, due to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Marsh Marigold include reducing inflammation, acting as an antiseptic, and having antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of eczema, acne, and other skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Marsh Marigold can be made by infusing the flowers in hot water to create a tea or by using the plant's roots and leaves in a tincture or salve.
